REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANTS WILLIAM WONG, WWJW FAMILY'S LLC DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED, WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. CALIFORNIA RECOGNIZES THE EXISTENCE OF A TRUST RESTING ON PAROL EVIDENCE, WHERE A TRUSTEE HAS COMMITTED FRAUD. (SEE TONEY V. NOLDER (1985) 173 CAL.APP.3D. 791, 796.) PLAINTIFF MUST PLEAD ACTUAL OR CONSTRUCTIVE FRAUD WITH SPECIFICITY. (TINDELL V. MURPHY (2018) 22 CAL.APP.5TH 1239.) IN PARTICULAR, PLAINTIFF'S COMPLAINT SHOULD MINIMALLY CONTAIN SPECIFIC FACTUAL ALLEGATIONS OF THE DATE, INVOLVED PARTIES, AND CIRCUMSTANCES CONCERNING THE CREATION OF A FIDUCIARY RELATIONSHIP BETWEEN PLAINTIFF AND DEFENDANTS. AS TO THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION, PLAINTIFF MUST ALLEGE SPECIFIC ALLEGATIONS SHOWING HOW DEFENDANT WONG'S CONTROL OF SUBJECT PROPERTY AMOUNTED TO A CONTROLLING INFLUENCE OF TOM KIANG RESTAURANT, INC. THE DEMURRER HAS TO BE SUSTAINED AS TO THE REMAINING CLAIMS BECAUSE THEY ARE SUBSTANTIVELY TETHERED TO THE FIRST AND SECOND CAUSES OF ACTION. REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE IS GRANTED. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC3.1312(B). JUDGE CHARLES F. HAINES, CLERK: N. WONG, NOT REPORTED (D501)

Defendant Wwjw Family Llc Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Plaintiff Ton Kiang Restaurant, Inc.S Responses To Request For Production Of Documents, Set No. One, And For Sanctions
Off Calendar Feb-28-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES: Atty. KRISTIN WILLIAMS, ESQ. of Kirschenbaum Law PC (Tel: 510-740-9260) appearing for the plaintiff;
Atty. RYAN MAU, ESQ. of Law Office of Ryan Mau PC (Tel: 415-296-7700) appearing for defendants WWJW Family LLC and William Wong.

Hearing Re: Defendant Wwjw Family LLC's Motion To Compel Plaintiff Ton Kiang Restaurant, Inc.'s Responses To Request For Production Of Documents, Set No. One, And For Sanctions.

The Court's tentative ruling instructed the parties to appear. Both sides stipulate to the pro tem judge. The matter is argued and submitted. The Court rules as follows: Motion to compel is granted. Plaintiff to provide verified response as to documents produced and to provide a code compliance response to Request No. 12. Responses to be served no later than 10 days from notice of entry of order. Request for sanctions is granted in the amount of $960.00 to be paid no later than 10 days after notice of entry of order. Order signed in open court.

Judge Pro Tem: Noah Lebowitz; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/JPT)

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-06-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows: DEFENDANT WILLIAM WONG, WWJW FAMILY LLC DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED with leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege in good faith facts showing that per Father's directions William took title and Plaintiff took care of any and all funds involved in the transfer and any attendant debt servicing. (FAC, paragraph 11). Facts should include description of the "funds," "debt" and payments, if any. Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: J. Murphy; Not Reported. (501)

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-10-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTS WILLIAM WONG, WWJW FAMILY'S LLC DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED, with leave to amend. California recognizes the existence of a trust resting on parol evidence, where a trustee has committed fraud. (See Toney v. Nolder (1985) 173 Cal.App.3d. 791, 796.) Plaintiff must plead actual or constructive fraud with specificity. (Tindell v. Murphy (2018) 22 Cal.App.5th 1239.) In particular, plaintiff's complaint should minimally contain specific factual allegations of the date, involved parties, and circumstances concerning the creation of a fiduciary relationship between Plaintiff and Defendants. As to the second cause of action, Plaintiff must allege specific allegations showing how Defendant Wong's control of subject property amounted to a controlling influence of Tom Kiang Restaurant, Inc. The demurrer has to be sustained as to the remaining claims because they are substantively tethered to the first and second causes of action. Request for Judicial Notice is GRANTED. Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC3.1312(b). Judge Charles F. Haines, Clerk: N. Wong, Not reported (D501)
